title: Using the Cache
text: cache disabled default. Using cache enables speed code development, expense sometimes using --date data (careful). Correctness preferred default. However, can significantly faster use cache, especially long-running queries embedded scripts. cache controlled use_cache function, call load icdb: first argument use_cache either TRUE (turn cache) FALSE (turn ). second argument, optional, specifies default lifetime objects cache. cache lifetime amount time object (query result) allowed remain cache, deleted database server queried . ensure data updated least sometimes. can set lifetime lubridate duration; set 24 hours default, meaning cached objects expire one day. can tailor lifetime value appropriate database. cache enabled, calls run() automatically store query results behind scenes: run query , server queried. Instead, cached results used. can tell cache used looking hits column cache tibble – time use cached results, hits increment one. addition storing number hits, various pieces information stored, including last time used query, original write time, amount time query took execute (first time). cache know whether query cache? run() function uses show_query() get SQL query string. hashes string, produce value shown hash column cache tibble. Every time run query, cache checked see query already performed. important realise cached results used even data server changed. example, performed SQL query SELECT foo bar twice, second query use cached results even new values added column foo database. query changed, cache can look see cached result used. therefore important manage lifetime cached objects appropriately, order prevent use --date results scripts. mechanism used prevent --date results cache lifetime. default cache lifetime 24 hours, can set anything like. example , lifetime 5 seconds used. means cached results remain valid fo 5 seconds; , attempt use result removal cache, shown : set cache lifetime timescale short possible, still allowing conveniently develop scripts. sure, leave default setting 24 hours. means need refetch results start day, results remain valid throughout day. disable cache lifetime entirely – want , set large lifetime (e.g. 1 year), achieve effect. cache creates folder called cache/ working directory. cached results stored folder. can delete time – happen results need refetched.
code: library(dplyr) library(icdb) use_cache(TRUE, lifetime = lubridate::dhours(24)) ## Generate artificial data icdb_example() #> Written generated APC data to 'gendata/apc.db'  ## Connect to the artifical database srv <- server(config = system.file("extdata", "sqlite.yaml", package="icdb")) #> Connecting using config file #> $dbname #> [1] "gendata/apc.db" #>  #> $drv #> <SQLiteDriver> #>  #> $bigint #> [1] "character"  ## Show the cache contents -- before you do anything, it will be empty show_cache() #> # A tibble: 0 × 7 #> # … with 7 variables: hash <chr>, data <chr>, hits <dbl>, write_time <date>, #> #   last_access <date>, time <drtn>, in_memory <lgl>                                         ## Perform a query (this will write results to the cache)  srv$APC_SYNTH %>% select(NHSNumber, HospitalProviderSpellIdentifier) %>% run() #> # A tibble: 405 × 2 #>    NHSNumber  HospitalProviderSpellIdentifier #>    <chr>                                <int> #>  1 9182076353                               1 #>  2 9182076353                               1 #>  3 9182076353                               1 #>  4 9182076353                               1 #>  5 2948013764                               2 #>  6 9340756123                               3 #>  7 9340756123                               3 #>  8 9340756123                               3 #>  9 9340756123                               3 #> 10 9340756123                               3 #> # … with 395 more rows  ## Look at the cache again -- now there is one entry show_cache() #> # A tibble: 1 × 7 #>   hash         data   hits write_time          last_access         time  in_me…¹ #>   <chr>        <chr> <dbl> <dttm>              <dttm>              <drt> <lgl>   #> 1 a7e17be4bcf… "\u0…     1 2022-11-27 12:19:43 2022-11-27 12:19:43 0.01… TRUE    #> # … with abbreviated variable name ¹​in_memory ## Do the same query again srv$APC_SYNTH %>% select(NHSNumber, HospitalProviderSpellIdentifier) %>% run() #> Found cached results for this query, using that #> # A tibble: 405 × 2 #>    NHSNumber  HospitalProviderSpellIdentifier #>    <chr>                                <int> #>  1 9182076353                               1 #>  2 9182076353                               1 #>  3 9182076353                               1 #>  4 9182076353                               1 #>  5 2948013764                               2 #>  6 9340756123                               3 #>  7 9340756123                               3 #>  8 9340756123                               3 #>  9 9340756123                               3 #> 10 9340756123                               3 #> # … with 395 more rows  ## Look at the hits column show_cache() #> # A tibble: 1 × 7 #>   hash         data   hits write_time          last_access         time  in_me…¹ #>   <chr>        <chr> <dbl> <dttm>              <dttm>              <drt> <lgl>   #> 1 a7e17be4bcf… "\u0…     2 2022-11-27 12:19:43 2022-11-27 12:19:43 0.01… TRUE    #> # … with abbreviated variable name ¹​in_memory ## Set a new default lifetime use_cache(TRUE, lifetime = lubridate::dseconds(5))  ## Perform a query srv$APC_SYNTH %>% select(NHSNumber, HospitalProviderSpellIdentifier) %>% run() #> Found cached results for this query, using that #> # A tibble: 405 × 2 #>    NHSNumber  HospitalProviderSpellIdentifier #>    <chr>                                <int> #>  1 9182076353                               1 #>  2 9182076353                               1 #>  3 9182076353                               1 #>  4 9182076353                               1 #>  5 2948013764                               2 #>  6 9340756123                               3 #>  7 9340756123                               3 #>  8 9340756123                               3 #>  9 9340756123                               3 #> 10 9340756123                               3 #> # … with 395 more rows  ## Wait for longer than 5 seconds Sys.sleep(10)  ## Do the same query again -- the cached result will not be used srv$APC_SYNTH %>% select(NHSNumber, HospitalProviderSpellIdentifier) %>% run() #> Cached object has expired, removing from the cache #> # A tibble: 405 × 2 #>    NHSNumber  HospitalProviderSpellIdentifier #>    <chr>                                <int> #>  1 9182076353                               1 #>  2 9182076353                               1 #>  3 9182076353                               1 #>  4 9182076353                               1 #>  5 2948013764                               2 #>  6 9340756123                               3 #>  7 9340756123                               3 #>  8 9340756123                               3 #>  9 9340756123                               3 #> 10 9340756123                               3 #> # … with 395 more rows

title: Using the Cache
text: Although cache automatically manages contents based lifetime, also possible manually remove elements cache, using clear_cache() function. simplest thing can delete everything cache: Clearing cache sometimes helpful, calling use_cache(FALSE) automatically clear cache. may want temporarily turn cache reason, delete large cached queries. also possible clear particular objects cache, according criteria specify . , pass cache tibble objects wish delete clear_cache(); can get cache tibble show_cache(). example, following code removes elements cache one hit: may use dplyr filter functions reduce cache tibble; just make sure result column names, rows strict subset tibble returned show_cache().
code: clear_cache() #> Cleared cache. show_cache() #> # A tibble: 0 × 7 #> # … with 7 variables: hash <chr>, data <chr>, hits <dbl>, write_time <date>, #> #   last_access <date>, time <drtn>, in_memory <lgl> show_cache() %>% filter(hits == 1) %>% clear_cache()

title: Using the Cache
text: section describes detail cache behaves. cache formed two parts: memory cache; disk cache. memory cache faster (query results load quickly), whereas disk cache persists across restarts R. disk cache stored cache/ directory R working directory, ensure use working directory want cache persist across R sessions. can see cached objects memory cache disk cache looking in_memory column cache tibble. query fetched first time, results placed memory cache. queries fetched, number queries memory cache increases. exceeds predefined size, results memory cache demoted disk cache. query demoted first one accessed last. Currently, close R session, contents memory cache flushed disk. means may see queries fetched expecting see cache. behaviour fixed future library version. details coming soon…

title: Contributing
text: issues using package, please log GitHub Issues Page. currently extremely likely issues: vast majority package underdeveloped mostly untested. However, feel brave enough try use , greatly value feedback/suggestions.

title: Contributing
text: idea main branch always works. branch protected, meaning possible directly push . Instead, must create pull request, meeting requirements : R CMD Check must pass (devtools::check()) Tests pass, test coverage must exceed 60% (number increase time) documentation must build successfully

what: How to get code into main
title: Contributing
text: Follow steps make changes repository: Create new branch, date copy main (pull current state main pre-existing branch, already ). making sure branch date possible main, make subsequent pull request easier (new branch similar main) Make changes local branch Run devtools::check() devtools::build_site() ensure code passes checks documentation works. fail, pull request succeed. finished making changes, open pull request GitHub. automatic checks passed, able merge changes main. Note spend quite branch, consider periodically pulling main branch performing required merges, branch remains close main.

what: Connecting to a Microsoft SQL server
title: Connection setup
text: easiest secure way connect database server hosting Microsoft SQL server use Windows ODBC Data Sources application. application stores pre-configured connection particular name (data source name), can used R code connect database. using type connection, store config files hard-coded database credentials. set connection: Open ODBC Data Sources program Click Add start adding new data source Select ODBC Driver 17 SQL server, SQL Server Native Client 11.0. Important: select SQL server driver. Click Finish. Choose arbitrary name (e.g. xsw) connection – data source name. Add optional description. server field, specify hostname server (see server Name field open SQL server Management Studio) Step pages wizard, leaving values default. end, make sure click Test Data Source… ensure connection working. step causes errors, make sure fixed moving . completing wizard, new data source listed ODBC Data Sources program. setting data source name, try executing code (replace xsw whatever used data source name): srv object behaves like list lists. first level contains databases server, next level contains tables database. Database table names tab complete (RStudio Emacs ESS), make easier find looking . running code , try typing srv$ see list database names. type srv$dbname$ get list tables database called dbname.
code: library(icdb) ## The next line may take a little while to run, due to the way the library works. srv <- server("xsw")

title: Connection setup
text: can connect MySQL server using configuration file formatted YAML. file must following structure: Replace fields information specific server, follows: driver: must set “mysql” connect MySQL server host: hostname MySQL server. name resolve IP address server. server running locally personal computer, set field “localhost”. user/password: need specify username password credentials accessing server. Th Save contents file, example mysql.yaml, put somewhere accessible R script. run following command Database names table names now tab complete typing srv$ srv$dbname. TOCHECK: also possible connect MySQL server using data source name, described section . Make sure follow data source wizard select MySQL server driver, specify connection credentials. TODO add detail .
code: --- "driver": "mysql" # Make sure you put a space after the colon "host": "mysql_server_hostname" "user": "test_mysql_user" "password": "Test_User%Password9" srv <- server(config = "path/to/mysql.yaml")

what: Connecting to an SQLite database file
title: Connection setup
text: can connect SQLite database file. Use configuration file (e.g. sqlite.yaml) following structure: Replace fields information specific server, follows: driver: must set “sqlite” connect SQLite database dbfile: path file containing SQLite database. path relative working directory R script create connection. run following command make connection:
code: ---  "driver": "sqlite" "dbfile": "database-file.db" ## You can view the working directory of the script like this. You need ## to set the dbfile field path relative to whatever directory is printed ## here getwd()  ## Make the connection srv <- server(config = "path/to/sqlite.yaml")

title: Development
text: section contains notes development, todo lists, etc.

title: Development
text: development environment compatible Windows, Linux Mac (todo check Mac). set R environment, install R install devtools package. can use R studio editor/IDE. top level directory R package, compatible devtools::load_all(). Tauri/Next.js development, need install dependencies. First, install Rust programming language operating system; give Rust compiler cargo package manager. Windows, install Node.js . Linux Mac, install Node Version Manager (NVM) instead, running: use install Node follows: Node.js installed, npm command; check running npm -v. cloned repository, change map-editor directory run installation dependencies first-time compilation may take bit time.
code: # Pick the latest version curl -o- https://raw.githubusercontent.com/nvm-sh/nvm/v0.39.1/install.sh | bash nvm install latest # Install node dependencies npm install  # Install cargo tauri command cargo install tauri-cli  # Install dependencies npm install @tauri-apps/api  # Build and run cargo tauri dev

title: Development
text: list important things: Fix cache flush level 2 cache end session (also refactor R6) list improvements need made: Connect server, database. Arrange things user can autocomplete databases tables. Try make work database connection can used multiple dplyr pipe operations. Flush level1 cache disk point (.e. session ends) shorten load time next session. Make sure clear way disable (might desirable default). Need try get autocomplete working every context makes sense (col names etc.). Look replacing dbSendQuery dbGetQuery simplicity Really need find way lazily evaluate contents Databases object. Currently, databases tables stored current autocomplete method rests built-autocompletion lists – however, requires list populated. better find different autocomplete method allowed lazy evaluation completion options. Need reconcile mysql sql server (microsoft) way getting lists databases tables. mysql returns objects properly using dbListObjects, sql server lists databases table clear way get tables without raw sql. Need make better error message user tries get non-existent table Need test cache properly Need add proper database disconnection code replace server connection files YAML JSON (consistency everything else) Replace testdata flag proper logic connecting SQLite database. title: ICD Code Management
text: input ICDB library, controls parsing interpretation ICD codes, codes mapping file, following format: file defined set nested “categories” blocks. blocks defines set codes, contains documentation describing category , may contain categories block. codes mapping file YAML format.
code: categories:   cond-1::     docs: "This is condition 1"     codes: ["I2%","I3%"]     categories:       sub-cond-1:         docs: "A subcondition"         codes: "I233"       sub-cond-2:         docs: "Another condition"         codes: ["I234", "I245"]   cond-2:     docs: "Something else"     codes: "I444"

title: ICD Code Management
text: ICDB library contains utilities automatically generating codes mapping files ICD codes. section describes set local copy https://icd..int/icdapi docker image, shows use source generate mapping file containing ICD codes.

title: Get Started
text: first step using library connecting database server. See Connection Setup page detailed information . page use artificial admitted patient care database, contains randomly generated data. can run examples without needing real database connection. First, generate artificial data (sqlite file), follows: create folder called gendata working directory, containing database file test.db. Next, can connect database using srv object behaves like nested named list. first level contains databases server, next level contains tables database. Database table names tab complete (RStudio Emacs ESS), make easier find looking . example , database contains single table called APC_SYNTH:
code: library(icdb) icdb_example() #> Written generated APC data to 'gendata/apc.db' library(icdb) ## The next line may take a little while to run for ## large databases, due to the way the library works. srv <- server(config = system.file("extdata", "sqlite.yaml", package="icdb")) #> Connecting using config file #> $dbname #> [1] "gendata/apc.db" #>  #> $drv #> <SQLiteDriver> #>  #> $bigint #> [1] "character" srv #> [1] "APC_SYNTH"

text: simplest use ICDB inspecting structure table. running expression returns tibble get run dplyr::tbl DBI database connection. object full table – just shell object showing columns data types table contains.
code: ## Print the structure of the table APC_SYNTH srv$APC_SYNTH #> # Source:   table<`APC_SYNTH`> [?? x 4] #> # Database: sqlite 3.39.4 [/home/runner/work/icdb/icdb/vignettes/gendata/apc.db] #>    NHSNumber  PrimaryDiagnosis_ICD HospitalProviderSpellIdentifier StartTime_H…¹ #>    <chr>      <chr>                                          <int> <chr>         #>  1 9182076353 K264                                               1 2019-05-24 0… #>  2 9182076353 I212                                               1 2019-05-24 0… #>  3 9182076353 I609                                               1 2019-05-24 0… #>  4 9182076353 I600                                               1 2019-05-24 0… #>  5 2948013764 K270                                               2 2018-11-05 2… #>  6 9340756123 K226                                               3 2019-09-15 1… #>  7 9340756123 K252                                               3 2019-09-15 1… #>  8 9340756123 I214                                               3 2019-09-15 1… #>  9 9340756123 K5793                                              3 2019-09-15 1… #> 10 9340756123 K5791                                              3 2019-09-15 1… #> # … with more rows, and abbreviated variable name #> #   ¹​StartTime_HospitalProviderSpell

title: Get Started
text: main advantage using dplyr (library ICDB uses) SQL often unnecessary, can replaced familiar dplyr functions. can pipe srv object dplyr functions like filter select get tibble data database. Try running snippet like following (replace valid database, table column names), shows SQL generated simple dplyr-based query: actually run query, replace show_query() run(): results (tibble), can anything normally tibble (filter, select, mutate, etc.) run() function wrapper around dplyr::collect(), adds caching functionality query. described next section.
code: library(dplyr) library(lubridate)  srv$APC_SYNTH %>% filter(StartTime_HospitalProviderSpell > !!ymd("2019-1-1")) %>% select(NHSNumber, PrimaryDiagnosis_ICD) %>% show_query() #> <SQL> #> SELECT `NHSNumber`, `PrimaryDiagnosis_ICD` #> FROM `APC_SYNTH` #> WHERE (`StartTime_HospitalProviderSpell` > '2019-01-01') # This returns a tibble containing the results of the query srv$APC_SYNTH %>% filter(StartTime_HospitalProviderSpell > !!ymd("2019-1-1")) %>% select(NHSNumber, PrimaryDiagnosis_ICD) %>% run() #> # A tibble: 190 × 2 #>    NHSNumber  PrimaryDiagnosis_ICD #>    <chr>      <chr>                #>  1 9182076353 K264                 #>  2 9182076353 I212                 #>  3 9182076353 I609                 #>  4 9182076353 I600                 #>  5 9340756123 K226                 #>  6 9340756123 K252                 #>  7 9340756123 I214                 #>  8 9340756123 K5793                #>  9 9340756123 K5791                #> 10 5376108293 I211                 #> # … with 180 more rows

what: Caching for speeding up code development
title: Get Started
text: Large queries (returning lot data, requiring lot server-side processing) can take long time execute. can make running scripts difficult, query script takes minutes run. ICDB library contains simple query cache helps speed queries development purposes. cache disabled default. enable , add following line top script query like run, may take several minutes execute. However, second time run, results returned instantly. run() function stores result behind scenes, case need soon. change aspect query changes SQL, query rerun. example, run new query, filter condition changed. However, go back old query, still stored cache. cache automatically deleted R session closed. can view current state cache running return tibble containing stored query metadata, including information write time last access. clear cache (delete stored queries), run cache uses combination memory disk caching, cached queries automatically expire (default) 24 hours. See Query Caching page detailed information cache works can control .
code: use_cache(TRUE) results <- srv$APC_SYNTH %>% filter(StartTime_HospitalProviderSpell > !!ymd("2019-1-1")) %>% select(NHSNumber, PrimaryDiagnosis_ICD) %>% run() results <- srv$APC_SYNTH %>% filter(StartTime_HospitalProviderSpell > !!ymd("2019-1-1")) %>% select(NHSNumber, PrimaryDiagnosis_ICD) %>% run() #> Found cached results for this query, using that # Returns a tibble where each row is a stored query show_cache() #> # A tibble: 1 × 7 #>   hash         data   hits write_time          last_access         time  in_me…¹ #>   <chr>        <chr> <dbl> <dttm>              <dttm>              <drt> <lgl>   #> 1 555ec4bfa74… "\u0…     2 2022-11-27 12:20:11 2022-11-27 12:20:11 0.02… TRUE    #> # … with abbreviated variable name ¹​in_memory # Returns a tibble where each row is a stored query clear_cache() #> Cleared cache.

text: state TRUE turn cache , FALSE turn lifetime default amount time cache results remain valid. Specified lubridate duration (e.g. lubridate::dhours(24)), default value 24 hours. size Use parameter set size level 1 (memory) cache, maximum number cached queries can stored older queries get flushed disk. default size 5.

title: Turn the cache on or off — use_cache
text: caching enabled, perform exactly query (example, running script), cached results used, speed running script. can make development debugging easier, turnaround time running commands scripts reduced. caching disabled, results always come database. can make code take longer run, always guarantee results date. cache disabled default. ensure , default, queries always return --date results. cache enabled, cached results automatically expire certain amount time, can specified using lifetime parameter. default cache lifetime 24 hours. Cached values expired automatically deleted cache attempt made fetch . expiry time based time cached results stored, cached result last accessed. purpose expiry ensure data cache refreshed least often lifetime.

title: icdb 0.0.0.9001
text: Added NEWS.md file track changes package. Added mapped database table functions. Added code definition file parser. Fixed missing dependency information dbplyr DESCRIPTION (fixing install problem README) Updated cache use memory disk. Disabled cache default. Call use_cache(TRUE) use cache. Added cache lifetime configurable level 1 size. Renamed Databases Server. Started adding tests Started proper package documentation site
